2604.05943.title: MARL-GPT: Foundation Model for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
2604.05943.paper_summary: Recent advances in multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) have demonstrated success in numerous challenging domains and environments, but typically require specialized models for each task. In this work, we propose a coherent methodology that makes it possible for a single GPT-based model to learn and perform well across diverse MARL environments and tasks, including StarCraft Multi-Agent Challenge, Google Research Football and POGEMA. Our method, MARL-GPT, applies offline reinforcement learning to train at scale on the expert trajectories (400M for SMACv2, 100M for GRF, and 1B for POGEMA) combined with a single transformer-based observation encoder that requires no task-specific tuning. Experiments show that MARL-GPT achieves competitive performance compared to specialized baselines in all tested environments. Thus, our findings suggest that it is, indeed, possible to build a multi-task transformer-based model for a wide variety of (significantly different) multi-agent problems paving the way to the fundamental MARL model (akin to ChatGPT, Llama, Mistral etc. in natural language modeling).
2604.05943.paper_summary_zh: 最近在多智能體強化學習（MARL）方面的進展已在眾多具有挑戰性的領域和環境中取得成功，但通常需要為每個任務專門設計模型。在這項工作中，我們提出了一種連貫的方法，使單一基於GPT的模型能夠在多樣的MARL環境和任務中學習並表現良好，包括星際爭霸多智能體挑戰、谷歌研究足球和POGEMA。我們的方法MARL-GPT應用離線強化學習，在專家軌跡上進行大規模訓練（SMACv2為4億，GRF為1億，POGEMA為10億），並結合一個不需要特定任務調整的單一Transformer基於觀察的編碼器。實驗顯示，MARL-GPT在所有測試環境中達到了與專門基準相媲美的性能。因此，我們的研究結果表明，確實有可能為各種（顯著不同的）多智能體問題構建一個多任務的基於Transformer的模型，為基本的MARL模型鋪平道路（類似於ChatGPT、Llama、Mistral等在自然語言建模中的應用）。
2604.05943.author: Maria Nesterova et.al.
2604.05943.authors: Maria Nesterova, Mikhail Kolosov, Anton Andreychuk, Egor Cherepanov, Oleg Bulichev, Alexey Kovalev, Konstantin Yakovlev, Aleksandr Panov, Alexey Skrynnik
